revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23991619004|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[en:Ikeda Tsuneoki]][[fa:ایکدا تسونه‌اوکی]][[fr:Ikeda Tsuneoki]][[it:Ikeda Tsuneoki]][[ja:池田恒興]][[ko:이케다 쓰네오키]][[pt:Ikeda Tsuneoki]][[th:อิเกดะ สึเนโอกิ]][[vi:Ikeda Tsuneoki]][[zh:池田恆興]] {{Infobox person|image=File:Ikeda Tsuneoki.jpg|birth_date=1536|death_date={{death date|1584|5|18}}|birth_place={{ill|Owari Province|qid=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}|death_place={{ill|Nagakute|qid=Q930396|1=Nagakute}}|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|qid=Q17|1=Japan}}|occupation={{ill|samurai|qid=Q38142|1=Samurai}}|title={{ill|daimyo|qid=Q61982|1=Daimyo}}|spouse={{ill|Zen'ōin|qid=Q60452149|1=Zen'ōin}}|children={{ill|Ikeda Motosuke|qid=Q837166|1=Ikeda Motosuke}}; {{ill|Ikeda Nagamasa|qid=Q11552186|1=Ikeda Nagamasa}}; {{ill|Ikeda Nagayoshi|qid=Q11552181|1=Ikeda Nagayoshi}}; {{ill|Ikeda Sen|qid=Q16846524|1=Ikeda Sen}}; {{ill|Ikeda Terumasa|qid=Q1385927|1=Ikeda Terumasa}}; {{ill|Q108860940|qid=Q108860940|1=Q108860940}}; {{ill|Q109657183|qid=Q109657183|1=Q109657183}}; {{ill|Q109657534|qid=Q109657534|1=Q109657534}}; {{ill|Waka-mandokoro|qid=Q60452174|1=Waka-mandokoro}}|father={{ill|Ikeda Tsunetoshi|qid=Q17192875|1=Ikeda Tsunetoshi}}|mother={{ill|Yōtoku-in|qid=Q11666829|1=Yōtoku-in}}|native_name=池田 恒興}} {{family name hatnote|{{ill|Ikeda clan|en|Ikeda clan|qid=Q2975277|lt=Ikeda|1=Ikeda clan}}|lang=Japanese}} {{Infobox officeholder | order = Head of {{ill|Ikeda clan|en|Ikeda clan|qid=Q2975277|lt=Ikeda clan|1=Ikeda clan}} | predecessor = Ikeda Tsunetoshi | successor = {{ill|Ikeda Terumasa|en|Ikeda Terumasa|qid=Q1385927|lt=Ikeda Terumasa|1=Ikeda Terumasa}} | term_start = 1538 | term_end = 1584 | order1 = Lord of {{ill|Inuyama Castle|en|Inuyama Castle|qid=Q1155484|lt=Inuyama Castle|1=Inuyama Castle}} | predecessor1 = Oda Nobukiyo | successor1 = Ishikawa Sadakiyo | term_start1 = 1570 | term_end1 = 1583 | order2 = Lord of {{ill|Ōgaki Domain|en|Ōgaki Domain|qid=Q839430|lt=Ōgaki|1=Ōgaki Domain}} | predecessor2 = Ujiie Naomasa | successor2 = {{ill|Toyotomi Hidetsugu|en|Toyotomi Hidetsugu|qid=Q967501|lt=Toyotomi Hidetsugu|1=Toyotomi Hidetsugu}} | term_start2 = 1583 | term_end2 = 1584 | name = Ikeda Nobuteru | native_name = 池田 恒興 | birth_date = 1536 | death_date = {{death date and age|1584|05|18|1536}} | birth_place = {{ill|Owari Province|en|Owari Province|qid=Q514506|lt=Owari Province|1=Owari Province}} | death_place = [[:en:Nagakute, Aichi|Nagakute, Aichi]] | image = Ikeda Tsuneoki at Hayashibara Museum of Art.jpg | caption = | allegiance = [[File:Mon-Oda.png|15px]] {{ill|Oda clan|en|Oda clan|qid=Q1132077|lt=Oda clan|1=Oda clan}}
[[File:Goshichi no kiri inverted.svg|15px]] {{ill|Toyotomi clan|en|Toyotomi clan|qid=Q1521658|lt=Toyotomi clan|1=Toyotomi clan}} | Rank = {{ill|Karō|en|Karō|qid=Q1316445|lt=Karō|1=Karō}}, [[Daimyo]] | unit = [[File:Japanese Crest Bizenn Chou.svg|15px]] {{ill|Ikeda clan|en|Ikeda clan|qid=Q2975277|lt=Mino-Ikeda family|1=Ikeda clan}} | commands = {{ill|Inuyama Castle|en|Inuyama Castle|qid=Q1155484|lt=Inuyama Castle|1=Inuyama Castle}}
{{ill|Ōgaki Castle|en|Ōgaki Castle|qid=Q1067359|lt=Ōgaki Castle|1=Ōgaki Castle}} | spouse = Zen'ōin | battles = {{ill|Battle of Inō|en|Battle of Inō|qid=Q4187960|lt=Battle of Inō|1=Battle of Inō}}
{{ill|Suemori Castle|en|Suemori Castle|qid=Q846681|lt=Battle of Suemori|1=Suemori Castle}}
{{ill|Battle of Okehazama|en|Battle of Okehazama|qid=Q349422|lt=Battle of Okehazama|1=Battle of Okehazama}}
[[:en:Siege of Inabayama|Siege of Inabayama]]
{{ill|Battle of Anegawa|en|Battle of Anegawa|qid=Q964983|lt=Battle of Anegawa|1=Battle of Anegawa}}
{{ill|Siege of Mount Hiei|en|Siege of Mount Hiei|qid=Q10748887|lt=Siege of Mount Hiei|1=Siege of Mount Hiei}}
[[Siege of Makishima]]
{{ill|Battle of Nagashino|en|Battle of Nagashino|qid=Q428369|lt=Battle of Nagashino|1=Battle of Nagashino}}
{{ill|Battle of Tedorigawa|en|Battle of Tedorigawa|qid=Q1045768|lt=Battle of Tedorigawa|1=Battle of Tedorigawa}}
[[Siege of Hanakuma]]
{{ill|Battle of Yamazaki|en|Battle of Yamazaki|qid=Q39040|lt=Battle of Yamazaki|1=Battle of Yamazaki}}
{{ill|Battle of Shizugatake|en|Battle of Shizugatake|qid=Q225562|lt=Battle of Shizugatake|1=Battle of Shizugatake}}
{{ill|Battle of Komaki and Nagakute|en|Battle of Komaki and Nagakute|qid=Q1353633|lt=Battle of Komaki and Nagakute|1=Battle of Komaki and Nagakute}} | nickname = "Tsuneoki"
"Shōzaburō" | relations = [[Mori Nagayoshi]] (son in law) }} {{nihongo|'''Ikeda Tsuneoki'''|池田 恒興|extra= 1536 – May 18, 1584}}, also known as '''Ikeda Nobuteru''' (池田 信輝), was an {{ill|Ikeda clan|en|Ikeda clan|qid=Q2975277|lt=Ikeda clan|1=Ikeda clan}} ''[[:en:Daimyō|Daimyō]]'' and military commander under {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|en|Oda Nobunaga|qid=Q171411|lt=Oda Nobunaga|1=Oda Nobunaga}} during the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|qid=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}} and {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|en|Azuchi–Momoyama period|qid=Q319531|lt=Azuchi–Momoyama period|1=Azuchi–Momoyama period}}s of 16th-century Japan. He was a retainer of the famous warlords {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|en|Oda Nobunaga|qid=Q171411|lt=Oda Nobunaga|1=Oda Nobunaga}} and {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qid=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}. In his early years, he served Nobunaga since his mother was Nobunaga's [[:en:foster mother|foster mother]]. ==Early life== His childhood name is unknown and his common name was Katsuzaburō (勝三郎). His father was Ikeda Tsunetoshi, who served {{ill|Oda Nobuhide|en|Oda Nobuhide|qid=Q1153962|lt=Oda Nobuhide|1=Oda Nobuhide}}. His mother, Yōtokuin (養徳院), was {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|en|Oda Nobunaga|qid=Q171411|lt=Oda Nobunaga|1=Oda Nobunaga}}'s wet nurse, and later became Nobuhide's concubine, bearing him a daughter. There are various theories as to his birthplace (including the {{ill|Owari Province|en|Owari Province|qid=Q514506|lt=Owari|1=Owari Province}}, {{ill|Mino Province|en|Mino Province|qid=Q864156|lt=Mino|1=Mino Province}}, {{ill|Settsu Province|en|Settsu Province|qid=Q864163|lt=Settsu|1=Settsu Province}} and {{ill|Ōmi Province|en|Ōmi Province|qid=Q843882|lt=Ōmi|1=Ōmi Province}} {{ill|Provinces of Japan|en|Provinces of Japan|qid=Q860290|lt=provinces|1=Provinces of Japan}}). He was one of the four ''{{ill|Karō|en|Karō|qid=Q1316445|lt=Karō|1=Karō}}'' at {{ill|Kiyosu Castle|en|Kiyosu Castle|qid=Q1357421|lt=Kiyosu Castle|1=Kiyosu Castle}}. His official position was ''Kii-no-[[:en:Kokushi (officials)|kami]]'' (紀伊守), or "Governor of {{ill|Kii Province|en|Kii Province|qid=Q864053|lt=Kii Province|1=Kii Province}}". His {{ill|Courtesy name|en|Courtesy name|qid=Q2075910|lt=Courtesy name|1=Courtesy name}} was '''Shōzaburō''' (勝三郎). He later became a priest and referred to himself as '''Shōnyū''' (勝入). ==Military life== In 1556, he defeated {{ill|Oda Nobuyuki|en|Oda Nobuyuki|qid=Q1186776|lt=Oda Nobuyuki|1=Oda Nobuyuki}}, who was guilty of treason against his brother {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|en|Oda Nobunaga|qid=Q171411|lt=Oda Nobunaga|1=Oda Nobunaga}}.{{cite book|last1=Turnbull|first1=Stephen|title=The Samurai Sourcebook|date=1998|publisher=Cassell & Co|location=London|isbn=9781854095237|page=69}} In 1558, he took over {{ill|Suemori Castle|en|Suemori Castle|qid=Q846681|lt=Suemori Castle|1=Suemori Castle}}. In 1560, he led one of Nobunaga's main forces against {{ill|Imagawa Yoshimoto|en|Imagawa Yoshimoto|qid=Q1054305|lt=Imagawa Yoshimoto|1=Imagawa Yoshimoto}} at the {{ill|Battle of Okehazama|en|Battle of Okehazama|qid=Q349422|lt=Battle of Okehazama|1=Battle of Okehazama}}. In 1567, he participated in the {{ill|Siege of Inabayama Castle|en|Siege of Inabayama Castle|qid=Q5962857|lt=Siege of Inabayama Castle|1=Siege of Inabayama Castle}} against the [[:en:Saito clan|Saitō clan]] in the first division of Oda Nobunaga's forces, along with {{ill|Shibata Katsuie|en|Shibata Katsuie|qid=Q707587|lt=Shibata Katsuie|1=Shibata Katsuie}}. In 1570, he was active in the {{ill|Battle of Anegawa|en|Battle of Anegawa|qid=Q964983|lt=Battle of Anegawa|1=Battle of Anegawa}} against the {{ill|Azai clan|en|Azai clan|qid=Q1872031|lt=Azai|1=Azai clan}}-{{ill|Asakura clan|en|Asakura clan|qid=Q1772267|lt=Asakura|1=Asakura clan}} alliance and became the lord of {{ill|Inuyama Castle|en|Inuyama Castle|qid=Q1155484|lt=Inuyama Castle|1=Inuyama Castle}}. In 1571, Tsuneoki took part in the burning {{ill|Siege of Mount Hiei|en|Siege of Mount Hiei|qid=Q10748887|lt=Siege of Mount Hiei|1=Siege of Mount Hiei}} to subdue the followers of the Ishiyama-Honganji; attacks against the Nagashima Ikkō-ikki. In 1573, he participated in the [[Siege of Makishima]] Castle against {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshiaki|en|Ashikaga Yoshiaki|qid=Q434415|lt=Ashikaga Yoshiaki|1=Ashikaga Yoshiaki}}. Yoshiaki surrendered, and sending Yoshihiro, his eldest son, as a hostage to Nobunaga. In 1574, he entered the mountain fortress of [[Ori Castle]] in eastern Mino to pin-down Akechi Castle after it was taken by [[Takeda Katsuyori]]. In 1575, he fought in the {{ill|Battle of Nagashino|en|Battle of Nagashino|qid=Q428369|lt=Battle of Nagashino|1=Battle of Nagashino}} against the [[Takeda clan]]. In 1577, he took part in the {{ill|Battle of Tedorigawa|en|Battle of Tedorigawa|qid=Q1045768|lt=Battle of Tedorigawa|1=Battle of Tedorigawa}} against the [[Uesugi clan]]. In 1580, he beat {{ill|Araki Murashige|en|Araki Murashige|qid=Q2859472|lt=Araki Murashige|1=Araki Murashige}} at [[Siege of Hanakuma]] Castle, who locked himself in the castle and was given Murashige's domain at {{ill|Settsu Province|en|Settsu Province|qid=Q864163|lt=Settsu Province|1=Settsu Province}}. In 1582, he led 4000 troops, acted as the spearhead in the right wing, and became the chief vassal of Oda family in {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qid=Q187550|lt=Hashiba Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}'s force at the {{ill|Battle of Yamazaki|en|Battle of Yamazaki|qid=Q39040|lt=Battle of Yamazaki|1=Battle of Yamazaki}} after the [[:en:Incident at Honnō-ji|Incident at Honnō-ji]], helping defeat {{ill|Akechi Mitsuhide|en|Akechi Mitsuhide|qid=Q313320|lt=Akechi Mitsuhide|1=Akechi Mitsuhide}}.{{rp|277}} He was also in the conference at {{ill|Kiyosu Castle|en|Kiyosu Castle|qid=Q1357421|lt=Kiyosu Castle|1=Kiyosu Castle}} to decide on Nobunaga's successor. In 1583, he assisted Hideyoshi at {{ill|Battle of Shizugatake|en|Battle of Shizugatake|qid=Q225562|lt=Battle of Shizugatake|1=Battle of Shizugatake}} against {{ill|Shibata Katsuie|en|Shibata Katsuie|qid=Q707587|lt=Shibata Katsuie|1=Shibata Katsuie}} and was given 130,000 ''{{ill|Koku|en|Koku|qid=Q855048|lt=Koku|1=Koku}}'' in {{ill|Mino Province|en|Mino Province|qid=Q864156|lt=Mino Province|1=Mino Province}}, and became the lord of {{ill|Ōgaki Castle|en|Ōgaki Castle|qid=Q1067359|lt=Ōgaki Castle|1=Ōgaki Castle}}. In 1584, he fought in the {{ill|Battle of Komaki and Nagakute|en|Battle of Komaki and Nagakute|qid=Q1353633|lt=Battle of Komaki and Nagakute|1=Battle of Komaki and Nagakute}} on the side of Hideyoshi. He captured {{ill|Inuyama Castle|en|Inuyama Castle|qid=Q1155484|lt=Inuyama Castle|1=Inuyama Castle}} on his first assault. ==Death== [[File:Grave of Ikeda Tsuneoki in Ikeda.jpg|thumb|Grave of Ikeda Tsuneoki in Ikeda Gifu]] In May 1584, during {{ill|Battle of Komaki and Nagakute|en|Battle of Komaki and Nagakute|qid=Q1353633|lt=Battle of Komaki and Nagakute|1=Battle of Komaki and Nagakute}}, Tsuneoki, his eldest son, Ikeda Motosuke, and his son-in-law, [[Mori Nagayoshi]], were killed by {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|qid=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}}'s forces in the Battle at Nagakute.{{rp|43}} His daughter, {{ill|Ikeda Sen|en|Ikeda Sen|qid=Q16846524|lt=Ikeda Sen|1=Ikeda Sen}}, and his son, {{ill|Ikeda Terumasa|en|Ikeda Terumasa|qid=Q1385927|lt=Ikeda Terumasa|1=Ikeda Terumasa}}, survived the battle. Ikeda Terumasa, his second son, succeeded him as the new head of the {{ill|Ikeda clan|en|Ikeda clan|qid=Q2975277|lt=Ikeda clan|1=Ikeda clan}}. ==Family== * Father: Ikeda Tsunetoshi (d. 1538) * Mother: Yotokuin (1515–1608) * Wife: Zen'ōin * Concubines * Children: ** Ikeda Motosuke (1559–1584) by Zen'ōin ** {{ill|Ikeda Sen|en|Ikeda Sen|qid=Q16846524|lt=Senhime|1=Ikeda Sen}} (?-1599), married [[Mori Nagayoshi]], later married Nakamura Kazuuji ** {{ill|Ikeda Terumasa|en|Ikeda Terumasa|qid=Q1385927|lt=Ikeda Terumasa|1=Ikeda Terumasa}} (1565-1613) by Zen'ōin ** Ikeda Nagayoshi (1570–1614) by Zen'ōin ** Ikeda Nagamasa (1575–1607) by Zen'ōin ** Waka-Mandokoro married {{ill|Toyotomi Hidetsugu|en|Toyotomi Hidetsugu|qid=Q967501|lt=Toyotomi Hidetsugu|1=Toyotomi Hidetsugu}} ** Tenkyuin married Yamazaki Iemori ** daughter married {{ill|Asano Yoshinaga|en|Asano Yoshinaga|qid=Q1143577|lt=Asano Yoshinaga|1=Asano Yoshinaga}} ** daughter married {{ill|Oda Katsunaga|en|Oda Katsunaga|qid=Q4220111|lt=Oda Katsunaga|1=Oda Katsunaga}} ==References== {{Reflist}} {{cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=HQTbDphPKmoC&q=Ikeda+Tsuneoki&pg=PA163|title=Hideyoshi|isbn=9780674390263|access-date=12 August 2017|last1=Berry|first1=Mary Elizabeth|year=1989|publisher=Harvard Univ Asia Center }} {{People of the Sengoku period|state=autocollapse}} [[en:Ikeda Tsuneoki]] {{Commons category|Ikeda Tsuneoki}} [[fa:ایکدا تسونه‌اوکی]] [[fr:Ikeda Tsuneoki]] [[it:Ikeda Tsuneoki]] [[ja:池田恒興]] [[ko:이케다 쓰네오키]] [[pt:Ikeda Tsuneoki]] [[th:อิเกดะ สึเนโอกิ]] [[vi:Ikeda Tsuneoki]] [[zh:池田恆興]] {{wikidata link|Q1036482}} [[Category:1584 deaths]] [[Category:Daimyo]] [[Category:Ikeda clan]] [[Category:Japanese warriors killed in battle]] [[Category:Oda retainers]] [[Category:Toyotomi retainers]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
